{"query":"OpenAI ends video generation development Sora closure statement robotics real-world tasks agentic AI April 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1372,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/video/openai-announces-it-is-shutting-down-video-platform-sora-259979333650","title":"OpenAI announces it is shutting down video platform Sora","content":"OpenAI announced that it was shutting down its video generation platform "Sora." A deal that Disney struck with the company in December which resulted in a $1 billion investment will be dissolved.
NBC News' Brian Cheung reports on the latest developments and what it means for the tech giant."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c3w3e467ewqo","title":"Sora: OpenAI closes AI video app and cancels $1bn Disney deal","content":"OpenAI closes Sora video-making app and cancels $1bn Disney deal
OpenAI has shut down its artificial intelligence (AI) video-generation app Sora less than two years after its unveiling made headlines for creating realistic clips based on simple prompts.
At the same time OpenAI will also wind down its $1bn content partnership with entertainment giant Disney, the BBC understands.
OpenAI told the BBC on Wednesday that it has discontinued Sora so that it can focus on other developments, such as robotics "that will help people solve real-world, physical tasks".
OpenAI said it is shutting down both its Sora consumer app and the internet-based platform that professional install to generate videos.
Image-making tools on ChatGPT have not been affected by Sora's closure, OpenAI said.
Since the platform's launch, Sora made $1.4m in global net in-app revenues, compared to $1.9bn over the same period for ChatGPT, according to data from Seema Shah, VP of insights at market intelligence firm Sensor Tower.
"Given OpenAI is still unprofitable and pressure from investors and rivals is growing, this is cash they likely decided they can't afford to continue burning as initial interest has faded," said Henry Ajder, an expert on AI and deepfakes."},{"url":"https://thenationaldesk.com/news/americas-news-now/ending-openais-sora-unlikely-to-slow-influx-of-ai-generated-video-online-experts-say-openai-disney-winding-down-deal-artificial-intelligence-video-misinformation-reality-hijacking-technology","title":"chevron-right","content":"(TNND) — OpenAI is discontinuing Sora, its app that allows users to generate artificial intelligence videos with text prompts.
But OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT, said it is winding down its partnership with Disney as it shutters the Sora app.
A Disney spokesperson, meanwhile, told TNND in a statement that it respects OpenAI’s decision to exit the video-generation business.
OpenAI said it would continue to prioritize similar research, especially as it pertains to robotics and helping people solve problems that require real-world interaction.
Schiff said Sora might be among the best-known AI video platforms, but users can still turn to Google Veo, Runway, Luma, Kling and others.
OpenAI had safety controls and watermarking in place, so the switch to other platforms might come with fewer guardrails, Schiff said."},{"url":"https://www.mindstudio.ai/blog/why-openai-killed-sora-ai-video-generation-future","title":"Why OpenAI Killed Sora and What It Means for AI Video Generation | MindStudio","content":"By December 2024, Runway had shipped Gen-3 Alpha.
One of the more revealing subplots in Sora’s story involves what happened with Hollywood.
This is exactly what MindStudio’s AI Media Workbench is designed to handle.
Text intelligence, reasoning, coding assistance, and agentic workflows are where the large enterprise contracts live.
OpenAI shut down Sora primarily for strategic and financial reasons.
The market continues to grow, with strong products from Runway, Kling AI, Google Veo, Pika, Luma Dream Machine, and others.
Google Veo 3, with its added native audio generation, represents a significant step forward and has substantial enterprise distribution through YouTube."}]}

A Disney spokesperson, meanwhile, told TNND in a statement that it respects OpenAI’s decision to exit the video-generation business. OpenAI is shutting down Sora, the video-generation technology the company unveiled in 2024, shocking entertainment executives with its ability to use artificial intelligence to quickly produce short videos that looked as if a Hollywood studio had made them. The agreement was considered a watershed for the tech industry and Hollywood, and some worried it was the first big step toward replacing the entertainment industry’s actors and creators with A.I. OpenAI is shutting down both its Sora consumer app and the internet service that moviemakers and other professionals used to generate images for movies, television and other media, the company said. In a statement to The New York Times, OpenAI said it would continue to use video-generation technologies behind the scenes as a way of teaching skills to robots.","score":0.7460839,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/cnet/posts/the-end-of-an-era-openai-confirmed-on-tuesday-that-it-is-discontinuing-its-once-/1322384053084784","title":"CNET - The end of an era 🪦 OpenAI confirmed on Tuesday...","content":"## CNET's Post. OpenAI confirmed on Tuesday that it is discontinuing its once-viral AI video app, Sora, but there’s no timeline on the app’s deprecation. As we focus and compute demand grows, the Sora research team continues to focus on world simulation research to advance robotics that will help people solve real-world, physical tasks,” an OpenAI spokesperson told CNET. OpenAI’s head of applications reportedly told employees earlier this month that it will cut down on “side quests” to focus on more core activities. Given that, it’s unsurprising to hear OpenAI say it’s going to pull the plug on Sora. There’s no confirmation yet on what this means for OpenAI’s $1 billion deal with Disney, which included licensing of over 200 Disney characters to appear on Sora.","score":0.67192334,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c3w3e467ewqo","title":"OpenAI closes AI video app and cancels $1bn Disney deal","content":"# OpenAI closes Sora video-making app and cancels $1bn Disney deal. OpenAI has shut down its artificial intelligence (AI) video-generation app Sora less than two years after its unveiling made headlines for creating realistic clips based on simple prompts. At the same time OpenAI will also wind down its $1bn content partnership with entertainment giant Disney, the BBC understands. A spokesperson for The Walt Disney Company said \"we respect OpenAI's decision to exit the video generation business and to shift its priorities elsewhere\". Disney will engage with other AI platforms to find ways to responsibly use the technology without infringing on intellectual property rights, a spokesperson said. OpenAI said it is shutting down both its Sora consumer app and the internet-based platform that professional install to generate videos. The BBC understands that with the closure of Sora, OpenAI will no longer focus on developing video-generation tools.
